response.justification: James Smith is a capable generalist software engineer with roughly 17 years of broad experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, but he falls significantly short of the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) profile. The role demands a director-level individual contributor with recognized, deep AI expertise and demonstrated cloud platform leadership; Smith's AI exposure is limited to a few months at re:cinq (starting April 2025) building MCP tooling, with no track record of shipping AI-powered products or platforms at scale. He holds only bachelor's degrees with no advanced AI education, and his career arc — senior/staff-level full-stack and backend roles — does not reflect the executive advisory presence, cloud strategy ownership, or thought leadership the position requires.
strengths: 20 years of polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, demonstrating strong technical breadth and adaptability
Genuine entrepreneurial spirit evidenced by founding GISCollective, open-source contributions, and community workshop leadership
Recent exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq signals awareness of the current AI developer tooling landscape
concerns: AI expertise is nascent (April 2025 to present) with no demonstrated history of building or shipping AI-powered products or platforms at any meaningful scale
No cloud strategy or cloud architecture experience at the level required; AWS CDK usage in a pricing team context does not constitute cloud platform leadership
Career seniority and scope — consistently senior/staff individual contributor on product teams — does not match the director-level IC advisory and thought leadership responsibilities of this role

key_factors.factor: Broad technical breadth and entrepreneurial history
